text:
I was walking down the street, feeling like autumn. Cool air brushed against my skin, turning my cheeks a bright crimson. A particular song played softly on the radio. Birds sang melancholically. Leaves rustled like gentle whispers.

I love autumn. Everything feels slowed down, peaceful. I think of my grandmother. We'd collect acorns together. I look for those little treasures and think how much she loved collecting them too.

Just then, I heard a tiny cry, like a baby bird. I followed the sound to a garden corner. A baby gray squirrel was stuck.

"Come here!"

I scooped him up carefully. His eyes weren't real scary. Just wide and trusting.

"I'm here. Don't worry."

I brought him down to the trunk. Owls live there. I shooed him inside. The owls didn't care.

Back on my feet again. I wondered why I liked autumn so much. That was it. That tiny creature. It was
